course.title: Build and Ship a Production Feed Aggregator in Go 1.26
course.description: Advanced Go backend learners ready to integrate feed standards, bounded XML and HTTP, PostgreSQL, scheduling, multi-instance concurrency, APIs, accessible presentation, security, testing, deployment, and recovery into one cumulative service. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate a cumulative multi-tenant rss 2.0 and atom 1.0 service from authorized discovery through publisher-friendly ingestion, postgresql storage, search, apis, and an accessible content-safe reader through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate go ownership, pgx pooling and transactions, migrations, conditional http, bounded xml, normalization, durable scheduling, fenced claims, worker pools, observability, security, testing, deployment, backup, and recovery through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate original deterministic browser models plus controlled go compiler, httptest, postgresql, race, fuzz, subprocess, container, load, accessibility, backup, restore, and release transfer gates through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five distinct stakeholder projects and a cumulative production defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

credential.title: Build and Ship a Production Feed Aggregator in Go 1.26 Certificate

modules.title: Reader Outcomes, Publisher Stewardship, and Service Charter
modules.description: A coalition asks for a blog aggregator but has not named its readers, publisher obligations, freshness promise, moderation boundary, accessibility needs, or evidence of usefulness. Learners cumulatively produce reader-centered feed-service charter through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define reader group, decision, task journey, freshness need, harm, and observable value before choosing architecture.
Define respectful fetch frequency, identification, caching, opt-out, correction, deletion, and incident contacts for publishers.
Separate discovery, fetch, parse, normalize, persist, schedule, serve, and present responsibilities with explicit ports.

modules.title: Go 1.26 Repository, pgx, Migrations, and Reproduction
modules.description: The service runs from an unknown revision with an unrecorded compiler, floating PostgreSQL libraries, ad hoc schema edits, warm caches, and no clean reproduction. Learners cumulatively produce reproducible Go feed-service repository baseline through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Capture revision, worktree, configuration, fixtures, secrets, migrations, licenses, and recovery anchor before editing.
Pin Go 1.26.5, pgx v5.10.0, goose v3.27.2, PostgreSQL 18.4, module sums, and supported platforms.
Keep command, application, domain, repository, fetch, parser, scheduler, HTTP, and presentation dependencies directed inward.

modules.title: Feed Discovery, URL Identity, Scope, and SSRF Boundary
modules.description: Users can submit arbitrary URLs, discovery follows every alternate link, redirects change authority silently, and hostname checks occur only before DNS resolution. Learners cumulatively produce authorized feed discovery and network-scope policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Distinguish a direct feed URL from HTML autodiscovery and require an explicit bounded discovery policy.
Admit only alternate links with supported RSS or Atom media types, resolve references against the response URL, and bound candidates.
Normalize only justified URI components while preserving distinctions that can identify different publisher resources.

modules.title: RSS 2.0, Atom 1.0, Namespaces, and Extension Policy
modules.description: The parser assumes RSS only, ignores Atom namespaces, treats optional fields as required, loses xml:base and language, and guesses extension meaning. Learners cumulatively produce standards-aware syndication vocabulary map through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Map RSS channel and item required, optional, repeated, GUID, enclosure, category, source, date, and extension constructs.
Map Atom feed and entry identifiers, authors, links, text constructs, content, categories, dates, xml:base, and xml:lang.
Compare XML expanded names rather than prefixes and preserve unknown extension provenance without inventing semantics.

modules.title: Bounded XML Decoding, Encodings, Entities, and Structural Security
modules.description: The service unmarshals an unbounded body, disables strict decoding to accept broken feeds, expands custom entities, ignores depth, and silently replaces unsupported encodings. Learners cumulatively produce strict bounded XML admission boundary through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Enforce compressed and decoded byte, token, element-depth, attribute, text, item, and duration limits before allocation grows unbounded.
Keep encoding/xml strict mode, inspect tokens, reject malformed nesting and trailing documents, and never install arbitrary entity maps.
Use an allowlisted CharsetReader, reconcile transport and XML declarations, and reject unsupported or conflicting encodings.

modules.title: Normalized Feed Domain, Content Variants, and Provenance
modules.description: RSS and Atom transport structs leak into storage, missing data is invented, HTML and text are conflated, source identifiers are discarded, and updates cannot be explained. Learners cumulatively produce loss-aware normalized feed and entry model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Convert format-specific transport values into validated feed, entry, person, link, category, content, and provenance domain types.
Choose entry identity from stable Atom ID, RSS GUID semantics, canonical link, or scoped content fingerprint with explicit precedence.
Preserve plain text, escaped HTML, XHTML, external content, summary, and missing content as distinct bounded states.

modules.title: Conditional HTTP Fetching, Redirects, Compression, and Publisher Budgets
modules.description: Every poll downloads a full feed, validators are overwritten on failure, redirects bypass scope, compressed bytes expand without limit, and retries synchronize across publishers. Learners cumulatively produce publisher-friendly conditional fetch client through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Store and send ETag and Last-Modified validators correctly, interpret 304 without parsing a body, and update validators only from admitted responses.
Classify 200, 304, redirect, temporary, permanent, auth, missing, rate-limit, and unexpected responses before XML admission.
Reapply scheme, authority, address, hop, credential, and loop policy at each redirect and record permanent moves separately.

modules.title: PostgreSQL 18 Domain Schema, Keys, Constraints, and Index Intent
modules.description: The database uses URLs as mutable primary keys, repeats publisher strings, permits cross-tenant references, lacks temporal constraints, and indexes without query evidence. Learners cumulatively produce constrained relational feed-service schema through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Model tenants, users, sources, feeds, subscriptions, entries, authors, categories, content versions, fetches, and reader state with clear ownership.
Use stable internal keys plus scoped external identities and preserve canonical URL changes without breaking references.
Encode nullability, checks, unique scopes, foreign keys, cascades, timestamps, states, and length budgets in PostgreSQL.

modules.title: Versioned Migrations, Expand-Contract Changes, and Rollback Evidence
modules.description: Developers edit production tables manually, migrations mix irreversible data loss with schema changes, deploys assume zero old binaries, and rollback has never run. Learners cumulatively produce rehearsed schema and data migration chain through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Keep ordered immutable migration files, checksums, ownership, lock behavior, and applied-state inspection under revision control.
Decide transaction boundaries from each PostgreSQL operation and separate concurrent index, long data, and lock-sensitive work.
Stage additive schema, backfill, dual-read or dual-write, validation, cutover, cleanup, and old-version compatibility.

modules.title: pgx Pooling, Query Contracts, Transactions, and Cancellation
modules.description: Handlers create pools, query rows are not closed, transactions use the wrong connection, contexts are replaced, SQL errors lose identity, and shutdown races active work. Learners cumulatively produce bounded pgx repository and unit-of-work layer through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Create one bounded pgxpool, verify startup deliberately, observe acquire wait, and close after admission and worker shutdown.
Use parameterized typed queries, explicit columns, row closure, iteration errors, cardinality expectations, and explainable mapping.
Run every unit-of-work statement on one transaction, defer rollback safely, commit once, and preserve the original cause.

modules.title: Source Registration, Subscriptions, Following, and Ownership
modules.description: Each user creates duplicate fetch jobs for the same feed, unfollow deletes shared data, ownership is ambiguous, and source verification happens outside a transaction. Learners cumulatively produce shared-source and tenant subscription model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Register a discovered source idempotently from normalized identity, provenance, scope decision, and initial fetch evidence.
Create and remove tenant-scoped subscriptions idempotently while preserving a shared publisher source and entry history.
Model pending, active, paused, moved, failing, disabled, deleted, and appeal states with allowed transitions.

modules.title: Entry Identity, Idempotent Upsert, Revisions, and Deletion Semantics
modules.description: Every poll inserts duplicates, title edits create new posts, reused GUIDs overwrite unrelated content, disappeared entries are deleted immediately, and revision causes vanish. Learners cumulatively produce idempotent versioned entry ingestion model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Bind external entry identity to feed scope and format semantics, detect collisions, and retain the raw identity basis.
Insert new, ignore unchanged, version changed, and quarantine conflicting entries atomically under unique constraints.
Store meaningful normalized revisions with source fetch, field diff, timestamps, and bounded retention instead of overwriting evidence.

modules.title: Fetch-to-Commit Ingestion Unit, Checkpoints, and Partial Failure
modules.description: Validators commit before entries, half a feed becomes visible, parse failure erases the last good state, metrics report success before commit, and retries duplicate side effects. Learners cumulatively produce atomic feed ingestion unit of work through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Model claimed, fetching, fetched, parsed, normalized, committing, committed, failed, canceled, and superseded states.
Commit feed metadata, entries, revisions, validators, next schedule, and audit record together or preserve the prior admitted version.
Checkpoint only replay-safe boundaries and bind each checkpoint to source, validator, parser version, schema, and attempt identity.

modules.title: Due Scheduling, Monotonic Clocks, Backoff, Jitter, and Pause Policy
modules.description: A ticker polls every feed at one interval, wall-clock jumps duplicate work, failures retry in lockstep, successful publishers never adapt, and paused sources remain due. Learners cumulatively produce deterministic adaptive polling scheduler through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Persist next attempt, last success, failure streak, publisher hints, pause state, and scheduling reason independently from process memory.
Inject wall and monotonic time roles, define inclusive due comparisons, and test forward, backward, and boundary changes.
Apply bounded exponential backoff with deterministic-testable jitter, reset rules, Retry-After, and maximum staleness escalation.

modules.title: PostgreSQL Work Claims, SKIP LOCKED, Leases, and Fencing
modules.description: Multiple instances poll one source, row locks span network calls, expired workers commit late results, leases renew silently after ownership loss, and queue fairness is unknown. Learners cumulatively produce multi-instance fenced fetch-claim protocol through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Claim a bounded due batch with ordered FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED work in a short transaction and release locks before network I/O.
Issue attempt and fencing tokens with owner, expiry, heartbeat budget, and state so stale workers cannot commit.
Finalize success or failure only when source, attempt, token, and current lease match in one affected-row check.

modules.title: Bounded Worker Pool, Origin Fairness, Cancellation, and Shutdown
modules.description: The scheduler launches one goroutine per source, results block after cancellation, one origin occupies all workers, panics leak claims, and shutdown exits before commits settle. Learners cumulatively produce bounded origin-aware ingestion worker pool through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Derive global, tenant, origin, database, memory, and queue budgets and enforce the minimum active constraint.
Assign producers, closers, receivers, buffers, and terminal states so sends, closes, and drains cannot race or block forever.
Prevent one publisher from consuming all slots while preserving useful throughput and explicit per-origin limits.

modules.title: HTTP API Resources, Representations, Errors, and Handler Layers
modules.description: Routes mirror tables, handlers parse, query, and render directly, status codes are ad hoc, response shapes drift, and request cancellation never reaches storage. Learners cumulatively produce consistent feed-reader HTTP API through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Design tenant, source, subscription, entry, collection, search, and reader-state resources around client decisions rather than tables.
Separate routing, authority, request admission, application command, repository, representation, and observability with narrow interfaces.
Apply safe and idempotent method semantics, success statuses, preconditions, redirects, and retry guidance consistently.

modules.title: Authentication, Authorization, Tenant Isolation, and Privacy
modules.description: User IDs arrive in headers, handlers authorize by route presence, shared source data leaks private follows, audit logs capture tokens and reading history, and deletion scope is unclear. Learners cumulatively produce tenant-safe identity and privacy boundary through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Verify configured token issuer, audience, algorithm, time, key rotation, and transport before constructing a minimal principal.
Authorize each command from principal, tenant, role, ownership, relationship, and current resource state inside the application boundary.
Require tenant scope in repository APIs, SQL predicates, unique keys, tests, metrics, exports, and administrative paths.

modules.title: Cursor Pagination, Ordering, Filters, and Snapshot Behavior
modules.description: Entry lists use offset only, equal timestamps reorder, inserts create duplicates between pages, cursor fields are trusted, filters bypass indexes, and end boundaries are ambiguous. Learners cumulatively produce stable bounded reader timeline query through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Choose a deterministic immutable-enough order with unique tie-breaker and document updates that can move entries.
Encode version, tenant, sort values, direction, filters, expiry, and integrity without exposing authority or accepting arbitrary SQL.
Build forward and backward keyset predicates that match sort direction, null policy, indexes, and limit-plus-one boundaries.

modules.title: PostgreSQL Full-Text Search, Ranking, Language, and Abuse Budgets
modules.description: Search concatenates SQL, indexes raw HTML, assumes one language, ranking is unexplained, snippets render unsafe markup, and broad queries consume unbounded work. Learners cumulatively produce bounded explainable entry search through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Build a provenance-aware tsvector from admitted title, summary, safe text content, author, and categories with weighting and language policy.
Convert bounded user input through an explicit plainto, websearch, or structured tsquery policy and reject pathological or empty requests.
Pair GIN and supporting indexes with representative EXPLAIN evidence, data scale, selectivity, write cost, and regression thresholds.

modules.title: Accessible Reader Surface, Untrusted Content, and Safe Links
modules.description: The UI injects feed HTML, opens deceptive links, depends on card color, loses focus after updates, hides source context, and forces infinite scroll. Learners cumulatively produce keyboard-first content-safe feed reader through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Treat titles, authors, summaries, HTML, images, categories, and URLs as untrusted publisher input through render-time safety boundaries.
Prefer safe text, use a tested allowlist sanitizer only where needed, rewrite links, block active content, and preserve readable structure.
Use one page heading, feed and entry landmarks, descriptive link text, metadata lists, dates, language, and source provenance.

modules.title: Read, Unread, Star, Tag, Bulk Action, and Conflict Semantics
modules.description: Reading an entry mutates global source state, bulk operations partially apply silently, device updates overwrite each other, tags leak across tenants, and unread counts drift. Learners cumulatively produce consistent personal reader-state model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Keep read, unread, starred, hidden, saved, and tag relationships scoped to principal, tenant, and entry without mutating shared content.
Make set-state commands idempotent with explicit desired state, preconditions where needed, and stable response semantics.
Define bounded all-or-nothing or per-item bulk semantics, authorization, result reconciliation, and retry behavior.

modules.title: Reader API Caching, ETags, Preconditions, and Invalidation
modules.description: Personal timelines are cached publicly, validators omit tenant and filters, 304 responses ignore authorization changes, writes leave stale pages, and weak and strong tags are confused. Learners cumulatively produce privacy-safe conditional reader API through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Classify public, tenant-private, user-private, sensitive, non-storeable, and invalidation-sensitive representations before fields are set.
Derive validators from authorized representation version, query, locale, content negotiation, and principal scope and choose weak or strong semantics.
Evaluate If-None-Match precedence and If-Modified-Since correctly after authentication and representation selection.

modules.title: Causal Observability, Freshness SLOs, Capacity, and Cost
modules.description: Logs contain feed bodies and tokens, traces lose attempt identity, averages hide stale publishers, metrics explode by URL, and load tests ignore PostgreSQL and origin budgets. Learners cumulatively produce privacy-minimized feed-service operations report through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Correlate request, tenant-safe source key, schedule claim, fetch attempt, validator, parse, transaction, and response without leaking raw private identifiers.
Emit bounded structured events with causal error identity and tested redaction for URLs, content, queries, headers, tokens, SQL, and personal state.
Define availability, successful-poll, publisher-respect, ingestion-lag, reader-latency, and restore indicators with windows and error budgets.

modules.title: Unit Fixtures, Properties, Fuzzing, Race Detection, and Leak Evidence
modules.description: Tests use one copied feed, assert only counts, contact public sites, share mutable clocks, skip malformed XML, and infer race freedom from normal runs. Learners cumulatively produce layered deterministic Go evidence suite through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Create small original RSS, Atom, namespace, encoding, date, identity, update, malformed, entity, oversized, and extension fixtures with explicit expected evidence.
Test URL, discovery, parse, normalize, schedule, state, cursor, search, and policy functions with injected clocks and deterministic inputs.
Fuzz XML tokens, URLs, dates, cursors, state transitions, and normalization with bounded invariants and preserved minimal failures.

modules.title: PostgreSQL Integration, Loopback HTTP, Fault Injection, and Reconciliation
modules.description: Mocks confirm SQL strings but not constraints, HTTP tests skip redirects and compression, retries never meet real transaction errors, and cleanup is not asserted. Learners cumulatively produce disposable end-to-end ingestion harness through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Start an isolated compatible PostgreSQL 18 database, apply real migrations, seed original data, isolate tests, and prove teardown.
Use httptest origins and custom transports for validators, redirects, DNS decisions, compression, slow bodies, malformed bytes, disconnects, and publisher budgets.
Inject constraint, serialization, deadlock, lock wait, pool exhaustion, cancellation, connection loss, and commit ambiguity with expected recovery.

modules.title: SSRF, XML, Content, Query, Abuse, Secrets, and Retention Defense
modules.description: Security is split across ad hoc checks, admin paths bypass URL policy, XML and HTML have different budgets, search supports denial of service, secrets enter logs, and data is kept forever. Learners cumulatively produce threat-modeled feed-service control set through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Trace assets, actors, trust boundaries, entry points, abuse cases, controls, detection, response, and residual risk across every data flow.
Combine URL grammar, allow or deny policy, address validation, redirect checks, proxy controls, egress isolation, metadata blocking, and rebinding tests.
Align wire, decompression, XML, text, HTML sanitation, search, pagination, bulk, and export limits so one stage cannot amplify another.

modules.title: Container Delivery, Health, Migration Coordination, and Safe Rollout
modules.description: The image runs as root, startup mutates schema on every replica, readiness ignores PostgreSQL and backlog, shutdown drops leases, and rollout lacks version compatibility. Learners cumulatively produce deployable Go feed-service release unit through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Build a minimal non-root pinned multi-stage image with exact binary, migrations, certificates, ownership, metadata, SBOM, signature, and vulnerability evidence.
Validate configuration, secrets references, database compatibility, migration state, network policy, budgets, and dependency reachability before serving.
Separate liveness, readiness, startup, degraded ingestion, backlog, database, and maintenance states without exposing secrets or causing restart loops.

modules.title: Backup, Point-in-Time Recovery, Rebuild, and Disaster Reconciliation
modules.description: Backups exist but are untested, feed content is assumed refetchable, reader state shares no recovery objective, restore replays workers early, and corruption remains hidden. Learners cumulatively produce rehearsed feed-service recovery system through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Assign RPO, RTO, retention, encryption, restore, and rebuild strategy separately to accounts, subscriptions, reader state, feeds, entries, raw evidence, and telemetry.
Verify scheduled backups, WAL continuity, encryption, access, checksums, catalog, age, capacity, and restore eligibility.
Restore into isolation, validate schema and application versions, reconcile counts and invariants, and prevent outbound fetching during inspection.

modules.title: Release Reconciliation, Incident Response, and Production Defense
modules.description: The team has many passing checks but cannot bind source to artifact, explain feed loss, prove publisher respect, restore service, or identify residual risks. Learners cumulatively produce defensible production Go feed aggregator through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Bind reviewed revision, generated code, migrations, dependency graph, tests, image digest, SBOM, signature, configuration, and deployment identity.
Demonstrate unfamiliar RSS and Atom sources from authorized discovery through bounded fetch, normalization, transaction, scheduling, API, accessible reader, and cleanup.
Triage freshness, publisher overload, SSRF, parser, database, tenant, content, search, rollout, and restore incidents with evidence-preserving containment.
